name: @elizaos/app-scape
description: 'scape — first-class agent integration for xRSPS. Autonomous RuneScape-alike agent with TOON-encoded state, Scape Journal, and directed-prompt operator control.

SCAPE_BOT_SDK_TOKEN.description: Shared secret for the xRSPS bot-SDK endpoint. Must match the server's BOT_SDK_TOKEN env var. Without this set, the plugin will not connect and the agent will not enter the world.

SCAPE_AGENT_PASSWORD.description: Password for the agent's xRSPS account. Leave blank to auto-generate a 24-char random password on first launch (persisted to ~/.eliza/scape-agent-identity.json and reused on every subsequent run). Only set this if you want to pin a specific existing account — and note that whatever value you set will be written to the identity file in plaintext, so do not reuse a production password.

SCAPE_BOT_SDK_URL.description: WebSocket URL of the xRSPS bot-SDK endpoint. Defaults to the production 'scape deployment at wss://scape-96cxt.sevalla.app/botsdk (same host as the main game, path-routed, TLS-terminated by Sevalla's ingress). Override to ws://127.0.0.1:8080/botsdk for local dev against the shared-HTTP topology, or ws://127.0.0.1:43595 for legacy standalone BOT_SDK_PORT.

SCAPE_CLIENT_URL.description: URL the /viewer iframe loads — the xRSPS React client. Defaults to the production 'scape deployment at https://scape-client-2sqyc.kinsta.page (React client with WS URL, OSRS cache URL, and map tiles all baked in at build time, pointing at the deployed game server). Override to http://localhost:3000 for local dev against the craco server.

SCAPE_AGENT_NAME.description: In-game display name for the agent. Becomes the account username (normalized, max 12 chars). Must not conflict with any human player's name.

SCAPE_AGENT_ID.description: Stable identifier across reconnects. Used as the journal filename. Defaults to 'scape-{SCAPE_AGENT_NAME}'.

SCAPE_AGENT_PERSONA.description: Optional persona string fed into the LLM's system prompt so the agent keeps a consistent voice across sessions.

SCAPE_LOOP_INTERVAL_MS.description: Autonomous LLM step interval in milliseconds. Lower = more expensive, more responsive. Minimum 1000.

SCAPE_MODEL_SIZE.description: Which model tier the autonomous loop uses. Smaller = cheaper, larger = smarter.
